name: sphinxawesome-theme

metadata.name: sphinxawesome-theme
metadata.summary: An awesome theme for the Sphinx documentation generator

documentation:
Sphinx awesome theme

Create beautiful and awesome documentation websites with Sphinx. See how the theme looks like on sphinxawesome.xyz.

## Get started 1. Install the theme as a Python package: ```console pip install sphinxawesome-theme ``` For more information, see [Install the theme](https://sphinxawesome.xyz/how-to/install/). 1. Add `sphinxawesome_theme` as an HTML theme in your Sphinx configuration file `conf.py`: ```python html_theme = "sphinxawesome_theme" ``` For more information, see [Add your theme](https://sphinxawesome.xyz/how-to/add/). ## Features With the Awesome Theme, you can build readable, functional, and beautiful documentation websites. ### Awesome code blocks - Code block have a **Copy** button for copying the code. - You can use these additional options in `code-block` directives: - `emphasize-added`. Highlight lines that should be added - `emphasize-removed`. Highlight lines that should be removed - `emphasize-text: TEXT`. Highlight _`TEXT`_ in the code block ### Better headerlinks Clicking the link icon after headers or captions automatically copies the URL to the clipboard. ### DocSearch This theme supports the [`sphinx-docsearch`](https://sphinx-docsearch.readthedocs.io/) extension to replace the built-in search with Algolia DocSearch.

:copyright: Copyright, Kai Welke.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.jinja_functions._make_canonical
documentation:
Turn a filepath into the correct canonical link.

Upstream Sphinx builds the wrong canonical links for the `dirhtml` builder.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.jinja_functions.setup_jinja

modules.name: sphinxawesome_theme.toc
documentation:
Manipulate the on-page TOC.

:copyright: Kai Welke.
:license: MIT

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.toc.findall
documentation:
A backwards-compatible method to traverse docutils nodes.

`findall` isn't available in docutils < 0.18.
This can be removed if we pin the minimum version of Sphinx to >5.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.toc.change_toc
documentation:
Change the way the `{{ toc }}` helper works. By default, Sphinx includes the page title in the on-page TOC. We don't want that. Sphinx returns the following structure:
  • Page title
    • H2 and below
We first remove the `title` node. This gives us:
    • H2 and below
Then, we _outdent_ the tree.

modules.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess
documentation:
Post-process the HTML produced by Sphinx.

Some modifications can be done more easily on the finished HTML.

This module defines a simple pipeline:

1. Read all HTML files
2. Parse them with `BeautifulSoup`
3. Perform a chain of actions on the tree in place

See the `_modify_html()` function for the list of
transformations.

Note: This file is not processed by Webpack; don't use Tailwind utility classes.
They might not show up in the final CSS.

:copyright: Copyright Kai Welke.
:license: MIT, see LICENSE.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.changed_docs
documentation:
Add a list of changed docs to the environment.

This is useful to make sure postprocessing only runs on changed files.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.get_html_files
documentation: Get a list of HTML files.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.collapsible_nav
documentation: Make navigation links with children collapsible.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.remove_empty_toctree
documentation:
Remove empty toctree divs.

If you include a `toctree` with the `hidden` option,
an empty `div` is inserted. Remove them.
The empty `div` contains a single `end-of-line` character.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.headerlinks
documentation: Make headerlinks copy their URL on click.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.scrollspy
documentation: Add an active class to current TOC links in the right sidebar.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.external_links
documentation:
Add `rel="nofollow noopener"` to external links.

The alternative was to copy `visit_reference` in the HTMLTranslator
and change literally one line.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.strip_comments
documentation: Remove HTML comments from documents.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.modify_html
documentation:
Modify a single HTML document.

1. The HTML document is parsed into a BeautifulSoup tree.
2. The modifications are performed in order and in place.
3. After these modifications, the HTML is written into a file,
overwriting the original file.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.post_process_html
documentation:
Modify the HTML after building.

Make some changes that are easier to do in the final HTML rather than
the tree of nodes.
This function requires, that the list of files to postprocess is in the
environment as `app.env.awesome_changed_docs`.

classes.name: sphinxawesome_theme.postprocess.Icons
documentation:
Icons from Material Design.

See: https://material.io/resources/icons/

modules.name: sphinxawesome_theme.jsonimpl
documentation:
Custom JSON serializer.

The awesome theme uses custom jinja2 helper functions which are
non-serializable by default. Hence, I need to use a custom JSON
serializer.

:copyright: Copyright Kai Welke.
:license: MIT, see LICENSE for details.

modules.name: sphinxawesome_theme.code
documentation:
Extend Sphinx's code-block directive.

New options:

- `:emphasize-added:`: highlight added lines
- `:emphasize-removed:`: highlight removed lines
- `:emphasize-text:`: highlight a single word, such as, a placeholder

```
copyright

Copyright Kai Welke.

license

MIT, see LICENSE for details.
```

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.code.setup
documentation: Set up extension.

classes.name: sphinxawesome_theme.code.AwesomeCodeBlock

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.code.AwesomeCodeBlock._get_line_numbers
documentation: Parse the line numbers for the `:emphasize-added:` and `:emphasize-removed:` options.

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.code.AwesomeCodeBlock._extra_args
documentation: Set extra attributes for line highlighting.

documentation: Handle parsing extra options for highlighting.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.code.AwesomeCodeBlock.option_spec
documentation: type: ignore[misc]

An extension of the Sphinx `code-block` directive to handle additional options.

- `:emphasize-added:` highlight added lines
- `:emphasize-removed:` highlight removed lines
- `:emphasize-text:` highlight placeholder text

The job of the directive is to set the correct options for the `literal_block` node,
which represents a code block in the parsed reStructuredText tree.
When transforming the abstract tree to HTML,
Sphinx passes these options to the `highlight_block` method,
which is a wrapper around Pygments' `highlight` method.
Handling these options is then a job of the `AwesomePygmentsBridge`.

modules.name: sphinxawesome_theme.deprecated
documentation:
Check for deprecated options.

This extension checks if you're using a deprecated option from the
sphinxawesome_theme from a version < 5.0.

Theme options in the `html_theme_options` dictionary are handled automatically.
Regular configuration options however need to be checked separately,
because the HTML theme is loaded \_after\_ the configuration is handled,
and extensions are already processed.

To load this extension, add:

```{code-block} python
:caption: '|conf|'

extensions += ["sphinxawesome_theme.deprecated"]
```

```
copyright

Kai Welke.

license

MIT, see LICENSE for details
```

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.deprecated.check_deprecated
documentation: Check the Sphinx configuration for the deprecated options and migrate them automatically if possible.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.deprecated.setup
documentation: Register the extension.

modules.name: sphinxawesome_theme.logos
documentation:
Support different light and dark mode logos.

By default, Sphinx provides `html_logo` as an option
to add a logo to your documentation project.

The Awesome Theme lets you define separate logos for light and dark mode
via theme options:

```python
html_theme_options = {
    "logo_light": "<path>/<filename>",
    "logo_dark": "<path>/<filename>"
}
```

Provide a path relative to the Sphinx configuration directory (with the {file}`conf.py` file).

```
copyright

Copyright Kai Welke.

license

MIT, see LICENSE for details
```

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.logos.get_theme_options
documentation:
Return theme options for the application.

Adapted from the `pydata_sphinx_theme`.
<https://github.com/pydata/pydata-sphinx-theme/blob/f15ecfed59a2a5096c05496a3d817fef4ef9a0af/src/pydata_sphinx_theme/utils.py>

classes.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.show_prev_next
documentation: If `True`, the theme includes links to the previous and next pages in the hierarchy.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.show_breadcrumbs
documentation: If `True`, the theme includes [breadcrumbs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Breadcrumb_navigation) links on every page except the root page.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.breadcrumbs_separator
documentation: The separator for the breadcrumbs links.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.awesome_headerlinks
documentation: If `True`, clicking a headerlink copies its URL to the clipboard.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.show_scrolltop
documentation: If `True`, the theme shows a button that scrolls to the top of the page when clicked.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.awesome_external_links
documentation: If `True`, the theme includes an icon after external links and adds `rel="nofollow noopener"` to the links' attributes.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.main_nav_links
documentation:
A dictionary with links to include in the site header.

The keys of this dictionary are the labels for the links.
The values are absolute or relative URLs.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.extra_header_link_icons
documentation:
A dictionary with extra icons to include on the right of the search bar.

The keys are labels for the link's `title` attribute.
The values are themselves {class}`LinkIcon`.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.logo_light
documentation: A path to a logo for the light mode. If you're using separate logos for light and dark mode, you **must** provide both logos.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.logo_dark
documentation: A path to a logo for the dark mode. If you're using separate logos for light and dark mode, you **must** provide both logos.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.globaltoc_includehidden
documentation:
If `True`, the theme includes entries from *hidden*
{sphinxdocs}`toctree <usage/restructuredtext/directives.html#directive-toctree>` directives in the sidebar.

The `toctree` directive generates a list of links on the page where you include it,
unless you set the `:hidden:` option.

This option is inherited from the `basic` theme.

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.nav_include_hidden
documentation:
Deprecated. Use `globaltoc_includehidden` instead.

:::{deprecated} 5.0
:::

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.show_nav
documentation:
Deprecated. Use the `html_sidebars` option instead.

:::{deprecated} 5.0
:::

class_variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.ThemeOptions.extra_header_links
documentation:
Deprecated. Use either `main_nav_links` or `extra_header_link_icons` instead.

:::{deprecated} 5.0
:::

Helper class for configuring the Awesome Theme.

Each attribute becomes a key in the {confval}`sphinx:html_theme_options` dictionary.

modules.name: sphinxawesome_theme.builder
documentation:
Override code from the default HTML builder related to pygments CSS.

This extension extends the default HTML builder by changing the way
Pygments CSS is handled.

- Add a `pygments_style_dark` configuration option.
- Append dark mode classes to the main `pygments.css` file,
  instead of writing them in a separate file,
  properly prepend all classes for dark mode with `.dark`

```
copyright

Copyright Kai Welke.

license

MIT, see LICENSE for details.
```

classes.name: sphinxawesome_theme.builder.AwesomeHTMLBuilder

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.builder.AwesomeHTMLBuilder.init_highlighter
documentation: Initialize Pygments highlighters.

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.builder.AwesomeHTMLBuilder.create_pygments_style_file
documentation: Create CSS file for Pygments.
HTML builder that overrides a few methods related to handling CSS for Pygments.

modules.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting
documentation:
Add more highlighting options to Pygments.

Provide a new Pygments HTML formatter `AwesomeHtmlFormatter`.
This handles formatting the lines for added or removed options.
This changes the output compared to the default Sphinx implementation.
For example, each line is wrapped in a `<span>` element,
and the whole code block is wrapped in a `<pre><code>..` element.
For highlighted lines, this extension uses `<mark>`, `<ins>`, and `<del>` elements.

Define a new custom Pygments filter `AwesomePlaceholders`,
which wraps each encountered placeholder word in a `Generic.Emphasized` token,
such that we can style placeholders by CSS.

```
copyright

Copyright Kai Welke.

license

MIT, see LICENSE for details.
```

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting._replace_placeholders
documentation: Replace every occurence of `regex` with `Generic.Emph` token.

functions.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.setup
documentation:
Set up extension.

This module is no longer needed as an extension and no code will be run.
The classes and methods are called from `__init__.py`.

variables.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.TokenType
documentation: For Python 3.8

classes.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomePlaceholders

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomePlaceholders.__init__
documentation: Create an instance of the `AwesomePlaceholders` filter.

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomePlaceholders.filter
documentation: Filter on all tokens.

A Pygments filter for marking up placeholder text.

You can define the text to highlight with the `hl_text` option.
To add the filter to a Pygments lexer, use the `add_filter` method:

```python
f = AwesomePlaceholders(hl_text=TEXT)
lexer.add_filter(AwesomePlaceholders(hl_text=TEXT))
```

For more information, see the [Pygments documentation](https://pygments.org/docs/quickstart/).

classes.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomeHtmlFormatter

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomeHtmlFormatter._get_line_numbers
documentation: Get the lines to be added or removed.

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomeHtmlFormatter.__init__
documentation:
Implement `hl_added` and `hl_removed` options.

Also set the `linespans` and `wrapcode` options of the Pygments HTML formatter to `True`.

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomeHtmlFormatter._highlight_lines
documentation:
Highlight added, removed, and emphasized lines.

In contrast to Pygments, use `<mark>`, `<ins>`, and `<del>` elements.

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomeHtmlFormatter.format_unencoded
documentation:
Overwrite method to handle emphasized, added, and removed lines.

Unfortunately, the method doesn't extend easily, so I copy it from Pygments.

Custom Pygments HTML formatter for highlighting added or removed lines.

The method is similar to handling the `hl_lines` option in the regular HtmlFormatter.

classes.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomePygmentsBridge

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomePygmentsBridge.get_lexer
documentation:
Extend the `PygmentsBridge.get_lexer` method.

Adds a filter to lexers if the `hl_text` option is present.

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomePygmentsBridge.highlight_block
documentation:
Extend the `PygmentsBridge.highlight_block` method.

This method is called when Sphinx transforms the abstract document tree to HTML and encounters code blocks.

methods.name: sphinxawesome_theme.highlighting.AwesomePygmentsBridge.get_stylesheet
documentation:
Override the `PygmentsBridge.get_stylesheet` method.

This lets you prepend all Pygments classes with a common prefix, such as `.dark`.
Extend the PygmentsBridge to handle highlighting placeholder text.
